rationale: In the citing paper, Andrews, Ainslie, and Currim (2002) appears in a grouped citation that lists prior hierarchical/MNL conjoint literature when introducing the general modeling context. The text does not single out this paper's specific findings, assumptions, or methods as a direct input to hypothesis development, model specification, estimation choices, or interpretation. There is no distinct discussion of its discrete-vs-continuous heterogeneity comparison in the citing paper's argument, so its role is background rather than functionally central.
